reports.title: UK Electricity Mix Weekly Report: Sunday 28 June
reports.intro: A measured weekly report for EnergyMix.info using the available 7-day historical generation feed for Monday 22 June to Saturday 27 June.
reports.summary: The week from Monday 22 June to Saturday 27 June averaged about 28.8 GW of measured generation, with renewables averaging 37.0% of measured output. The strongest renewable day in the available data was Saturday 27 June, when wind, solar and hydro together averaged 43.2% of measured generation. Gas was highest on Wednesday 24 June and lowest on Saturday 27 June.
reports.takeaway: For flexible electricity use, the cleanest opportunities are likely to appear when wind is strong, gas is low and demand is not at a peak. This is the foundation for future EV-charging guidance, clean-electricity alerts and weekly newsletter summaries.
drivers: Renewables were strongest on Saturday 27 June, when wind, solar and hydro averaged 43.2% of measured generation.
Wind was the largest swing factor in the available feed, peaking at about 6.4 GW average output on Saturday 27 June.
Solar was strongest on Thursday 25 June, averaging about 5.3 GW across settlement periods after embedded-solar backfill where needed.
Gas averaged about 12.1 GW across the period, rising highest on Wednesday 24 June and falling lowest on Saturday 27 June.
Measured generation was highest on Thursday 25 June, at roughly 30.5 GW average output.

cleanestPeriods.title: Best low-carbon proxy: Saturday 27 June
cleanestPeriods.body: Historical carbon-intensity aggregates are not yet part of this weekly report feed, so EnergyMix.info uses renewable share and gas output as cautious proxies. Saturday 27 June had the strongest renewable share at 43.2%.

cleanestPeriods.title: Lowest gas proxy: Saturday 27 June
cleanestPeriods.body: Gas generation averaged about 9.0 GW on Saturday 27 June. Lower gas periods are often cleaner, but the report does not yet claim this was the lowest-carbon period without historical carbon-intensity validation.

higherCarbonPeriods.title: Highest gas proxy: Wednesday 24 June
higherCarbonPeriods.body: Gas averaged about 14.4 GW on Wednesday 24 June. Gas-heavy periods are usually higher-carbon, but this is labelled as a proxy until historical carbon-intensity highs/lows are added to the report feed.

highlights.label: Wind
highlights.value: Highest average wind output was about 6.4 GW on Saturday 27 June.

highlights.label: Solar
highlights.value: Highest average solar output was about 5.3 GW on Thursday 25 June.

highlights.label: Gas
highlights.value: Highest average gas output was about 14.4 GW on Wednesday 24 June; lowest was about 9.0 GW on Saturday 27 June.

reports.methodologyNote: This report is generated from the available 7-day historical generation feed and validated against external public data. Carbon-intensity highs/lows and interconnector summaries should be treated as future additions unless they are present as validated historical aggregates.

yesterday.title: UK Electricity Mix Yesterday: Saturday 27 June
yesterday.summary: Yesterday, Saturday 27 June, measured average generation was about 25.4 GW. Renewables averaged 43.2% of measured generation, gas averaged 9.0 GW, wind averaged 6.4 GW and solar averaged 4.2 GW.

highestRenewableShare.text: Current measured 7-day high: 43.2% average renewable share on Saturday 27 June, based on the available historical generation feed.

highestWindGeneration.text: Current measured 7-day high: about 6.4 GW average wind output on Saturday 27 June, based on the available historical generation feed.

highestSolarGeneration.text: Current measured 7-day high: about 5.3 GW average solar output on Thursday 25 June, based on the available historical generation feed.

highestGasGeneration.text: Current measured 7-day high: about 14.4 GW average gas output on Wednesday 24 June, based on the available historical generation feed.

socialPosts.title: Monday weekly grid brief
socialPosts.body:
Last week in Britain’s electricity mix:

• Highest renewable share in the available feed: 43.2% on Saturday 27 June
• Highest average wind output: ~6.4 GW on Saturday 27 June
• Highest average solar output: ~5.3 GW on Thursday 25 June
• Highest average gas output: ~14.4 GW on Wednesday 24 June

The shift was clear: gas-heavy weekdays gave way to a cleaner, wind-led weekend.

Full report: https://energymix.info/reports/weekly/2026-06-28/

socialPosts.title: Wednesday explainer
socialPosts.body:
What does “UK electricity mix” actually mean?

For live grid dashboards, it usually means Great Britain’s electricity system: England, Scotland and Wales. Northern Ireland is part of the UK, but operates in a separate electricity market.

Explainer: https://energymix.info/uk-electricity-mix

socialPosts.title: Friday practical clean-electricity post
socialPosts.body:
The cleanest time to use electricity is not a fixed hour.

It changes with wind, solar, demand, imports and gas generation. Windy overnight periods and sunny middays can be much cleaner than still early-evening peaks.

Guide: https://energymix.info/cleanest-time-to-use-electricity
